## Purpose

The Procurement Management Plan establishes the framework for purchasing or acquiring products, services, or results needed for [[PROJECT_NAME]] from outside the project team. It defines how procurement processes will be managed from developing procurement documents through contract closure.

---

## Scope

This Procurement Management Plan covers:
- Make-or-buy analysis
- Procurement strategy and approach
- Vendor selection criteria and process
- Contract types and terms
- Procurement documentation
- Vendor management and oversight
- Contract administration
- Procurement risk management

This plan does NOT cover:
- Internal resource allocation (see Resource Management Plan)
- Detailed contract terms (see individual contracts)
- Legal contract review (see Legal/Procurement department)

---

## Objectives

1. **Value:** Obtain best value for project budget
2. **Quality:** Ensure procured items meet quality standards
3. **Timeliness:** Acquire resources when needed
4. **Compliance:** Adhere to procurement policies and regulations
5. **Risk Management:** Minimize procurement-related risks
6. **Relationship:** Maintain positive vendor relationships

---

## Instructions for Completion

1. **Requirements Analysis:** Identify all procurement needs
2. **Make-or-Buy Analysis:** Determine internal vs. external sourcing
3. **Strategy Development:** Define procurement approach
4. **Vendor Identification:** Identify potential vendors
5. **Selection Criteria:** Define evaluation criteria
6. **Process Definition:** Document procurement process
7. **Contract Planning:** Define contract types and terms
8. **Baseline:** Obtain approval and execute procurement

---

# 1. MAKE-OR-BUY ANALYSIS

## 1.1 Analysis Criteria

| Factor | Make (Internal) | Buy (External) |
|--------|-----------------|----------------|
| **Cost** | Lower labor cost, no margin | Vendor expertise, economies of scale |
| **Control** | Full control over quality/process | Less control, dependent on vendor |
| **Time** | May take longer to build capacity | Faster if vendor available |
| **Expertise** | May lack specialized skills | Access to specialized expertise |
| **Risk** | Internal resource risk | Vendor performance risk |
| **Capacity** | Limited by internal capacity | Scalable capacity |
| **Strategic** | Core competency protection | Focus on core competencies |

## 1.2 Make-or-Buy Decisions

| Item | Decision | Rationale | Approval |
|------|----------|-----------|----------|
| [[ITEM_1]] | Make | [[RATIONALE]] | [[NAME]] |
| [[ITEM_2]] | Buy | [[RATIONALE]] | [[NAME]] |
| [[ITEM_3]] | Buy | [[RATIONALE]] | [[NAME]] |

## 2.1 Procurement Approach

|---------------|----------|---------------|--------|
| **Professional Services** | Competitive bid | Time & Materials | [[TIMING]] |
| **Software Licenses** | Direct negotiation | Fixed price | [[TIMING]] |
| **Hardware/Equipment** | Competitive bid | Fixed price | [[TIMING]] |
| **Cloud Services** | Direct negotiation | Subscription | [[TIMING]] |
| **Specialized Consulting** | Sole source | Time & Materials | [[TIMING]] |

# 3. VENDOR SELECTION

## 3.1 Vendor Evaluation Criteria

### Technical Criteria (40%)

| Criterion | Weight | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| **Technical Capability** | 15% | Ability to deliver required solution |
| **Experience** | 10% | Relevant project experience |
| **Methodology** | 10% | Approach and processes |
| **Innovation** | 5% | Creative solutions and ideas |

### Commercial Criteria (30%)

| Criterion | Weight | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| **Price** | 20% | Total cost of ownership |
| **Value** | 10% | Price-to-value ratio |

### Qualitative Criteria (30%)

| Criterion | Weight | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| **References** | 10% | Quality of references |
| **Cultural Fit** | 10% | Alignment with values |
| **Responsiveness** | 10% | Communication and flexibility |

## 3.2 Vendor Shortlist

| Vendor | Capabilities | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------------|--------|-------|
| [[VENDOR_1]] | [[CAPABILITIES]] | [[STATUS]] | [[NOTES]] |
| [[VENDOR_2]] | [[CAPABILITIES]] | [[STATUS]] | [[NOTES]] |
| [[VENDOR_3]] | [[CAPABILITIES]] | [[STATUS]] | [[NOTES]] |

## 3.3 Selection Process

### Step 1: Requirements Definition
- Document technical requirements
- Define commercial terms
- Establish evaluation criteria

### Step 2: Market Research
- Identify potential vendors
- Issue Request for Information (RFI)
- Shortlist qualified vendors

### Step 3: Proposal Request
- Issue Request for Proposal (RFP)
- Conduct vendor briefings
- Receive and evaluate proposals

### Step 4: Evaluation
- Technical evaluation
- Commercial evaluation
- Reference checks
- Presentations/demos

### Step 5: Selection
- Score proposals
- Negotiate terms
- Select vendor
- Obtain approvals

### Step 6: Contracting
- Draft contract
- Legal review
- Execute contract

---

# 4. CONTRACT TYPES

## 4.1 Contract Type Selection

| Type | Description | When to Use | Risk Allocation |
|------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
| **Fixed Price (FP)** | Set price for defined scope | Clear requirements, low risk | Vendor bears cost risk |
| **Time & Materials (T&M)** | Actual time and costs | Unclear scope, high collaboration | Buyer bears cost risk |
| **Cost Plus** | Cost plus fee | Research/development | Buyer bears all risk |
| **Unit Price** | Price per unit | Repetitive items | Shared risk |
| **Retainer** | Monthly fee for services | Ongoing services | Buyer bears utilization risk |

## 4.2 Contract Terms

### Standard Terms

| Term | Standard | Negotiable |
|------|----------|------------|
| **Payment Terms** | Net 30 | Yes |
| **Warranty** | 12 months | Yes |
| **Liability Cap** | Contract value | Yes |
| **Termination** | 30 days notice | Yes |
| **IP Rights** | Work for hire | Case by case |
| **Confidentiality** | Mutual NDA | Standard |

### Project-Specific Terms

| Term | Requirement | Rationale |
|------|-------------|-----------|
| [[TERM_1]] | [[REQUIREMENT]] | [[RATIONALE]] |
| [[TERM_2]] | [[REQUIREMENT]] | [[RATIONALE]] |

---

# 5. PROCUREMENT PROCESS

## 5.1 Process Flow

```
Identify Need → Market Research → RFI/RFP → Evaluate → Negotiate → Award → Manage → Close
```

## 5.2 Procurement Documentation

### Request for Information (RFI)
**Purpose:** Gather vendor capabilities  
**Content:**
- Company overview
- Capabilities and experience
- Approach and methodology
- References

### Request for Proposal (RFP)
**Purpose:** Solicit detailed proposals  
**Content:**
- Project background
- Scope of work
- Technical requirements
- Commercial terms
- Evaluation criteria
- Proposal format
- Submission requirements

### Statement of Work (SOW)
**Purpose:** Define work to be performed  
**Content:**
- Scope description
- Deliverables
- Timeline
- Acceptance criteria
- Roles and responsibilities

## 5.3 Approval Authorities

| Value | Approver | Documentation |
|-------|----------|---------------|
| < [[AMOUNT]] | Project Manager | Email approval |
| [[AMOUNT]] - [[AMOUNT]] | Product Owner | Written request |
| [[AMOUNT]] - [[AMOUNT]] | Steering Committee | Business case |
| > [[AMOUNT]] | Executive Sponsor | Formal proposal |

---

# 6. VENDOR MANAGEMENT

## 6.1 Vendor Onboarding

| Step | Activity | Owner | Timing |
|------|----------|-------|--------|
| 1 | Contract execution | Procurement | Week 0 |
| 2 | Security review | Security | Week 0 |
| 3 | Access provisioning | IT | Week 1 |
| 4 | Kickoff meeting | PM | Week 1 |
| 5 | Integration planning | TL | Week 1-2 |

## 6.2 Performance Management

### Performance Metrics

| Metric | Target | Measurement | Frequency |
|--------|--------|-------------|-----------|
| **On-time Delivery** | 100% | Deliverable dates | Per milestone |
| **Quality** | > 95% | Acceptance rate | Per deliverable |
| **Responsiveness** | < 24 hours | Response time | Ongoing |
| **Budget Adherence** | ± 5% | Cost variance | Monthly |
| **Communication** | > 4.0/5.0 | Satisfaction | Quarterly |

### Performance Review Schedule

| Review | Frequency | Participants | Focus |
|--------|-----------|--------------|-------|
| **Operational Review** | Weekly | PM, Vendor PM | Progress, issues |
| **Performance Review** | Monthly | PM, Vendor Lead | Metrics, trends |
| **Strategic Review** | Quarterly | Leadership | Relationship, strategy |

## 6.3 Issue Management

| Issue Type | Escalation Path | Resolution Time |
|------------|-----------------|-----------------|
| **Operational** | PM ↔ Vendor PM | 48 hours |
| **Performance** | PM → Vendor Lead | 1 week |
| **Contractual** | PM → Procurement | 2 weeks |
| **Strategic** | Sponsor → Vendor Exec | 1 month |

---

# 7. CONTRACT ADMINISTRATION

## 7.1 Contract Monitoring

| Activity | Frequency | Owner | Output |
|----------|-----------|-------|--------|
| **Deliverable Tracking** | Continuous | PM | Status report |
| **Invoice Review** | Monthly | PM/Finance | Payment approval |
| **Change Management** | As needed | PM | Change orders |
| **Compliance Check** | Quarterly | PM | Compliance report |

## 7.2 Change Control

### Change Order Process

1. **Request:** Vendor or PM submits change request
2. **Impact Analysis:** Assess scope, schedule, cost impact
3. **Negotiation:** Negotiate terms of change
4. **Approval:** Obtain appropriate approvals
5. **Documentation:** Execute change order
6. **Implementation:** Implement change

### Change Order Template

```
CHANGE ORDER

CO Number: CO-[[NUMBER]]
Contract: [[CONTRACT_NUMBER]]
Date: [[DATE]]

Description of Change:
[[DESCRIPTION]]

Impact:
Scope: [[IMPACT]]
Schedule: [[IMPACT]]
Cost: [[AMOUNT]]

Approved By:
Vendor: _________________ Date: _______
Buyer: _________________ Date: _______
```

## 7.3 Payment Process

| Step | Activity | Owner | Timeline |
|------|----------|-------|----------|
| 1 | Deliverable acceptance | PM | Upon delivery |
| 2 | Invoice receipt | Finance | Upon submission |
| 3 | Invoice validation | PM | 5 days |
| 4 | Approval | PM/PO | 3 days |
| 5 | Payment processing | Finance | Per terms |

---

# 8. PROCUREMENT RISKS

## 8.1 Risk Identification

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| **Vendor Performance** | Medium | High | Clear SOW, penalties, monitoring |
| **Cost Overrun** | Medium | High | Fixed price, change control |
| **Schedule Delay** | Medium | High | Milestones, penalties, backup |
| **Quality Issues** | Low | High | Acceptance criteria, QA |
| **Contract Dispute** | Low | Medium | Clear terms, dispute resolution |
| **Vendor Bankruptcy** | Low | Critical | Financial vetting, backup vendors |

## 8.2 Risk Mitigation Strategies

| Strategy | Implementation |
|----------|----------------|
| **Vendor Diversification** | Multiple vendors for critical items |
| **Contract Protections** | SLAs, penalties, termination rights |
| **Performance Bonds** | For high-value contracts |
| **Regular Monitoring** | Frequent reviews and checkpoints |
| **Escrow Arrangements** | For critical source code |

---

# 9. ETHICS AND COMPLIANCE

## 9.1 Ethical Standards

- Fair and transparent vendor selection
- No conflicts of interest
- Equal treatment of all vendors
- Confidentiality of vendor information
- Honest communication

## 9.2 Compliance Requirements

| Requirement | Standard | Verification |
|-------------|----------|--------------|
| **Anti-corruption** | FCPA, local laws | Certification |
| **Data Protection** | GDPR, CCPA | Compliance review |
| **Labor Standards** | Local labor laws | Audit |
| **Environmental** | Environmental regulations | Certification |
| **Security** | Security standards | Security review |

---
